gloss.en: relating to managing and making business decisions

words.example: She has an executive position in a finance company.

gloss.en: waste gases sent out by an engine

words.example: Car exhaust pollutes the air in big cities.

gloss.en: making you feel very tired

words.example: The long hike was completely exhausting.

gloss.en: the state of being extremely tired

words.example: He collapsed from exhaustion after the race.

gloss.en: to show something in public

words.example: The artist will exhibit her paintings next month.

gloss.en: the state of being real or present

words.example: I was unaware of his existence until today.

gloss.en: present or in use now

words.example: We must improve the existing system first.

gloss.en: exciting and unusual, often from a foreign place

words.example: The garden was full of exotic flowers.

gloss.en: the act of becoming larger or greater

words.example: The company saw a period of rapid expansion.

gloss.en: a belief that something will likely happen

words.example: We have high expectation of a full recovery.

gloss.en: that you think will happen

words.example: Double the expected number of people came.

gloss.en: an organized journey with a special purpose

words.example: They planned an expedition to the South Pole.

gloss.en: the money you spend on something

words.example: The garden was transformed at great expense.

gloss.en: having or involving great knowledge or skill

words.example: We decided to seek expert advice.

gloss.en: expert knowledge or skill in a subject

words.example: We need technical expertise to fix this.

gloss.en: to burst loudly and violently

words.example: A second bomb exploded in a crowded market.

gloss.en: to use unfairly for your own gain

words.example: He exploited his father's name to get a job.

gloss.en: the act of travelling to find out about a place

words.example: Budgets for space exploration have been cut.

gloss.en: a person who travels to discover new places

words.example: The explorer mapped the unknown river.

gloss.en: the selling of goods to another country

words.example: There is a ban on the export of live cattle.

gloss.en: to show something that is usually hidden

words.example: He smiled, exposing a set of white teeth.

gloss.en: the state of being unprotected from something harmful

words.example: Prolonged exposure to radiation is dangerous.

gloss.en: clearly showing feelings or thoughts
gloss.zh: 富有表现力的；表情丰富的

words.example: She has a very expressive face.

gloss.en: extremely beautiful and finely made

words.example: She wore an exquisite silk dress.

gloss.en: to make something longer or larger

words.example: They plan to extend the road next year.

gloss.en: the act of making something larger or wider

words.example: The extension of new technology helped many.

gloss.en: covering a large area or amount

words.example: The house has extensive grounds.

gloss.en: in a way that covers a large area

words.example: She has travelled extensively.

gloss.en: how large or serious something is

words.example: It is hard to assess the full extent of the damage.

gloss.en: connected with the outside of something

words.example: The external walls of the building are stone.

gloss.en: in a very unusual or remarkable way

words.example: The view was extraordinarily beautiful.

gloss.en: unexpected, surprising or strange

words.example: What an extraordinary thing to say!

gloss.en: very great in degree

words.example: We are working under extreme pressure now.

gloss.en: very happy because of a success

words.example: The exultant team celebrated their big win.

gloss.en: the line of hair above each eye

words.example: She raised an eyebrow in surprise.

gloss.en: one of the small hairs on the edge of the eyelid

words.example: A long eyelash fell onto her cheek.

gloss.en: the piece of skin that covers the eye

words.example: He closed his eyelid and fell asleep.

gloss.en: cloth made by weaving wool, cotton, or silk

words.example: They sell a wide variety of cotton fabric.

gloss.en: extremely good

words.example: They put on a fabulous performance.

gloss.en: buildings or equipment provided for a purpose

words.example: The hotel has great sports facilities.

gloss.en: a building or service provided for a purpose

words.example: The new sports facility opens next week.

gloss.en: one of several things that cause something

words.example: Obesity is a major risk factor for heart disease.

gloss.en: not successful

words.example: He was a failed writer who never gave up.

gloss.en: lack of success in doing something

words.example: The success or failure of the plan depends on you.

gloss.en: trust in someone's ability or honesty

words.example: I have great faith in her judgement.

gloss.en: not real; made to look genuine

words.example: The stall was selling fake designer clothing.

gloss.en: to become weak or hesitate

words.example: Her voice began to falter as she spoke.

gloss.en: the state of being known by many people

words.example: The young singer achieved instant fame.

gloss.en: to learn about something so you know it well

words.example: Please familiarise yourself with the rules.

gloss.en: to learn about something so you know it well

words.example: Please familiarize yourself with the rules.

gloss.en: in an extremely good or extreme way

words.example: The team did fantastically well this season.

gloss.en: a pleasant situation you imagine

words.example: He spoke of his childhood fantasy of being famous.

gloss.en: at a greater distance away

words.example: I saw her on the far side of the road.

gloss.en: the money you pay to travel

words.example: Bus and taxi fares have gone up again.

gloss.en: very interested in something

words.example: The children were fascinated by the magic show.

gloss.en: a strong attraction or interest in something

words.example: He has a lifelong fascination with trains.

gloss.en: the father of your husband or wife

words.example: My father-in-law lives next door to us.

gloss.en: responsibility for something wrong

words.example: Why should I say sorry when it's not my fault?

gloss.en: not working correctly; having a fault

words.example: They returned the faulty washing machine.

gloss.en: a kind thing you do to help someone

words.example: Could you do me a favour and pick up Sam?

gloss.en: very frightening

words.example: The dragon had a fearsome roar.

gloss.en: one of the soft light parts covering a bird

words.example: She found a peacock feather in the park.

gloss.en: relating to a central government over states

words.example: They live in a federal republic.

gloss.en: money paid for professional advice or services

words.example: The lawyer charged a high fee for the advice.

gloss.en: a meal for a baby or an animal

words.example: The baby had her morning feed early today.

gloss.en: advice or information about how good something is

words.example: We collect customer feedback every month.

gloss.en: the sensation you get when you touch something

words.example: You can tell it's silk by the feel.

gloss.en: in the same situation or group as you

words.example: She helped her fellow students with the work.

gloss.en: a boat that carries people across water

words.example: We took a ferryboat across the wide river.

gloss.en: a body temperature higher than normal

words.example: He has a high fever and feels very weak.

gloss.en: a thread or material made of thin threads

words.example: This fruit is rich in fiber and vitamins.

gloss.en: invented; not real; from a story

words.example: The book follows a fictional detective.

gloss.en: the activity of battling an enemy

words.example: Fighting broke out in the city last night.

gloss.en: used in a symbolic, not literal, way

words.example: In a figurative sense, time can heal wounds.

gloss.en: a number representing an amount

words.example: The latest sales figures are encouraging.

gloss.en: a folder for keeping loose papers in order

words.example: She kept the documents in a box file.

gloss.en: relating to how a child treats a parent

words.example: In their culture, filial duty is very important.

gloss.en: money used to run a business or project

words.example: They must raise the necessary finance first.

gloss.en: in a way relating to money

words.example: The family is now financially secure.

gloss.en: information discovered through research

words.example: Our research finding surprised the whole team.

gloss.en: into very small pieces; very precisely

words.example: Chop the onion finely before cooking.

gloss.en: the hard part at the end of a finger

words.example: She painted each fingernail bright red.

gloss.en: a person whose job is to put out fires

words.example: A firefighter rescued the cat from the roof.

gloss.en: an open area in a room where a fire burns

words.example: We sat by the warm fireplace all evening.

gloss.en: a device that explodes with bright lights

words.example: They let off a firework to start the show.

gloss.en: fairly hard; not soft

words.example: I prefer to sleep on a firm bed.

gloss.en: in a strong or definite way

words.example: 'I can manage,' she said firmly.

gloss.en: the quality of being firm or strong

words.example: She spoke with great firmness and calm.

gloss.en: used to introduce the first point

words.example: Firstly, we need to agree on a plan.

gloss.en: relating to government money and taxes

words.example: The government announced a new fiscal policy.

gloss.en: a solution to a problem

words.example: Luckily there is an easy fix for this.

gloss.en: staying the same; not changing

words.example: These fixed prices give farmers some security.

gloss.en: a hot bright stream of burning gas

words.example: She lit the tiny yellow flame of a match.

gloss.en: to move wings or cloth up and down

words.example: The bird began to flap its wings.

gloss.en: a sudden bright light that quickly disappears

words.example: We saw a flash of lightning in the sky.

gloss.en: how food or drink tastes

words.example: The tomatoes give extra flavour to the sauce.

gloss.en: perfect; without any faults

words.example: The dancer gave a flawless performance.

gloss.en: a plant used to make linen and oil

words.example: Linen cloth is made from the flax plant.

gloss.en: able to change to suit new situations

words.example: We need a more flexible approach here.

gloss.en: in a way that can change easily

words.example: The team can work flexibly from home.

gloss.en: to turn something over quickly

words.example: She flipped the pancake in the pan.

gloss.en: to move slowly on water or in the air

words.example: A group of swans floated by the boat.

gloss.en: the ability to speak a language smoothly

words.example: She speaks French with great fluency.

gloss.en: a quick light movement

words.example: There was a flutter of wings in the tree.

gloss.en: the centre of interest or attention

words.example: It was the main focus of attention at the meeting.

gloss.en: a part of cloth that is folded or hangs

words.example: She smoothed the folds of her dress.

gloss.en: able to be folded for easy storage

words.example: He sat on a small folding chair.

gloss.en: a person who supports or admires someone

words.example: The leader had many loyal followers.

gloss.en: that is going to be mentioned next

words.example: Please answer the following questions.

gloss.en: a foolish act or lack of good sense

words.example: It would be folly to ignore the warning.

gloss.en: having warm or loving feelings for someone

words.example: Over the years, I have grown fond of her.

gloss.en: in a loving or affectionate way

words.example: She fondly remembered her school days.

gloss.en: a person who acts without good sense

words.example: Don't be such a fool!

gloss.en: in a way that lacks good sense

words.example: He foolishly left the door unlocked.

gloss.en: a note at the bottom of a page

words.example: The source is given in a footnote.

gloss.en: to order someone not to do something

words.example: The rules forbid running near the pool.

gloss.en: in a strong and powerful way

words.example: She argued forcefully for the change.

gloss.en: a statement about what will happen later

words.example: The sales forecast for next year is good.

gloss.en: a worker who leads a group of workers

words.example: The foreman checked the work on site.

gloss.en: to know about something before it happens

words.example: Nobody could foresee the sudden storm.
